column1: What is a class in Python?
column2: A blueprint for creating objects that bundles data (attributes) and behavior (methods)

A mechanism where a child class acquires attributes and methods from a parent class
The ability of different objects to respond to the same method call in their own way
Building complex objects by combining simpler objects (has-a relationship)
The constructor — called automatically when a new object is created
A reference to the current instance of the class
Hiding complex implementation details and exposing only the necessary interface
Don't Repeat Yourself — a principle to reduce code duplication
A function defined inside a class that operates on the object's data
A variable that belongs to an object or class
Hermann Ebbinghaus's model showing how memory decays exponentially over time without review
A learning technique that increases review intervals as material is better learned
A design principle that divides a program into distinct sections each handling a specific concern
A dictionary-like object that persists data across reruns of a Streamlit app
Comma-Separated Values — a plain-text file format for tabular data
A concise way to create lists: [expression for item in iterable if condition]
Returns the remainder of a division — used here for looping the card index
A Python function that shuffles a list in place using the Fisher-Yates algorithm
